auth_notes: Requires a Client ID and Client Secret to obtain a Bearer token via the /token endpoint. Access is typically restricted by IP allowlisting in production environments.
rate_limits: Standard limit is 100 requests per minute, though this varies based on the specific endpoint and enterprise contract.
gotchas: Payloads for loan applications are highly structured and require strict adherence to the Blend schema to avoid 400 errors.
Document uploads require handling multipart/form-data and specific metadata headers that can be tricky in the standard HTTP Request node.
Webhooks require a signature verification step to ensure authenticity, which must be handled via a Crypto node or Function node.
client_must_provide: API Environment Base URL (Sandbox vs Production)
research_notes: Integration will rely heavily on the HTTP Request node. Because Blend uses a standard OAuth2 Client Credentials flow, n8n's built-in OAuth2 credentials can be used. Developers should pay close attention to the 'X-Blend-Target-Instance' header if managing multiple environments or multi-tenant setups.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Integration will rely heavily on the HTTP Request node. Because Blend uses a standard OAuth2 Client Credentials flow, n8n's built-in OAuth2 credentials can be used. Developers should pay close attention to the 'X-Blend-Target-Instance' header if managing multiple environments or multi-tenant setups.
